# Component Inventory

## Purpose

This document is the single source of truth for all UI, layout, experience, motion, and infrastructure components used by the CTOS Portfolio.

Applies to:

- Human Developers
- Claude Code
- Codex
- Cursor Agent
- Roo Code
- Windsurf

---

# Component Philosophy

Every component must:

- Have a single responsibility
- Be reusable
- Be accessible
- Be documented
- Support the roadmap

---

# Component Classification

## Tier 1 — Foundation Components

Reusable primitives.

Examples:

- Button
- Input
- Dialog
- Drawer
- Tooltip
- Badge
- Tabs
- Accordion

Location:

src/components/ui/

---

## Tier 2 — Shared Components

Reusable portfolio components.

Examples:

- SectionHeader
- SectionContainer
- ThemeSwitcher
- LanguageSwitcher
- MagneticButton

Location:

src/components/shared/

---

## Tier 3 — Feature Components

Feature-specific components.

Examples:

- CommandPalette
- VisitorGlobe
- CertificateVault
- ResumeViewer

Location:

src/features/

---

## Tier 4 — Experience Components

High-value experience modules.

Examples:

- HeroSystemMap
- ArchitectureExplorer
- ProjectTimeline
- CaseStudyStory

Location:

src/features/experience/

---

# Layout Components

## RootLayout

Responsibility:

Global layout shell.

---

## ContentLayout

Responsibility:

Article and notes pages.

---

## CaseStudyLayout

Responsibility:

Case study presentation.

---

## ProjectLayout

Responsibility:

Project showcase pages.

---

# Navigation Components

## MainNavigation

Purpose:

Primary desktop navigation.

---

## MobileNavigation

Purpose:

Mobile experience navigation.

---

## CommandPalette

Purpose:

Global navigation and search.

Priority:

Critical

---

# Hero Components

## HeroSystemMap

Purpose:

Visual identity system.

Priority:

Critical

---

## HeroMetadata

Purpose:

Positioning information.

---

## HeroSignalLayer

Purpose:

Animated credibility indicators.

---

# Project Components

## ProjectCard

Purpose:

Project preview.

---

## ProjectGrid

Purpose:

Project listing.

---

## ProjectShowcase

Purpose:

Featured projects.

---

# Case Study Components

## CaseStudyCard

Purpose:

Case study preview.

---

## CaseStudyStory

Purpose:

Narrative storytelling.

---

## ArchitectureBreakdown

Purpose:

Technical explanation.

---

# Authority Components

## ProofSignalBar

Purpose:

Quick trust indicators.

---

## CertificateVault

Purpose:

Credential management.

---

## ResumeViewer

Purpose:

Resume preview and download.

---

# Contact Components

## ContactCard

Purpose:

Communication entry point.

---

## SocialLinks

Purpose:

Professional profile links.

---

# Motion Components

## MagneticButton

Purpose:

Micro interaction.

---

## CursorFollower

Purpose:

Premium interaction layer.

---

## MorphingSVG

Purpose:

Brand animation system.

---

# Search Components

## SearchDialog

Purpose:

Search interface.

---

## SearchResults

Purpose:

Search presentation.

---

# Analytics Components

## VisitorCounter

Purpose:

Traffic visibility.

---

## VisitorGlobe

Purpose:

Global visitor visualization.

---

# SEO Components

## SEOHead

Purpose:

Metadata management.

---

## StructuredData

Purpose:

JSON-LD generation.

---

# Accessibility Requirements

Every component must support:

- Keyboard Navigation
- Focus States
- Screen Readers
- Reduced Motion

---

# Component Lifecycle

1. Proposed
2. Approved
3. Implemented
4. Documented
5. Validated
6. Production

---

# Component Review Checklist

Before Production:

- Accessibility validated
- Performance validated
- Mobile validated
- Documentation updated
- No duplicate component exists

---

# AI Agent Rules

Rule CI-01

Reuse existing components before creating new ones.

Rule CI-02

Avoid duplicate responsibilities.

Rule CI-03

Document every new component.

Rule CI-04

Prefer composition over complexity.

Rule CI-05

Every component must map to a roadmap requirement.

---

# Definition Of Completion

A component is complete when:

- Implemented
- Accessible
- Reusable
- Documented
- Tested
- Approved for production
